 {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-17e","title":"EPIC: CI/CD & Quality Gates","description":"# Epic: CI/CD & Quality Gates

## Overview
Set up comprehensive CI/CD pipeline to ensure quality and enable confident deployments.

## Background & Reasoning
A project of this scope needs automated quality gates:
- Catch bugs before they reach users
- Verify installer actually works on real Ubuntu
- Ensure website builds correctly
- Maintain code quality standards

## CI Components

### 1. Code Quality
- ESLint for TypeScript/JavaScript
- Prettier for formatting
- shellcheck for bash scripts
- TypeScript type checking

### 2. Website Tests
- Unit tests for components
- Build verification
- Lighthouse CI for performance

### 3. Installer Tests
- Spin Ubuntu VM (GitHub Actions runner or cloud)
- Run full install.sh
- Execute acfs doctor
- Verify all tools present

### 4. Integration Tests
- Manifest parsing
- Generator outputs
- Doctor check coverage

## GitHub Actions Workflows

```yaml
# .github/workflows/ci.yml
- lint-and-type-check
- test-website
- test-installer-ubuntu-25
- shellcheck-scripts
```

## Success Criteria
- [ ] All PRs require passing CI
- [ ] Installer tested on real Ubuntu
- [ ] Website build verified
- [ ] shellcheck passes on all .sh files
- [ ] Coverage reports generated
- [ ] Deployment preview for PRs

## Considerations
- Keep CI fast (< 10 min for most checks)
- Installer test can be slower (separate workflow)
- Use caching aggressively
- Matrix test multiple Ubuntu versions","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-19T22:22:08.720517-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-20T12:47:02.195651-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-20T12:47:02.195651-05:00","close_reason":"Closed","labels":["ci","epic"]}
 {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-17e.1","title":"Set up GitHub Actions for website CI","description":"# Task: Set up GitHub Actions for Website CI

## Description
Create GitHub Actions workflow for website quality gates.

## Workflow File
`.github/workflows/website.yml`

## Jobs

### 1. lint-and-typecheck
```yaml
- uses: oven-sh/setup-bun@v1
- run: bun install
- run: bun run lint
- run: bun run type-check
```

### 2. build
```yaml
- uses: oven-sh/setup-bun@v1
- run: bun install
- run: bun run build
```

### 3. test (if tests exist)
```yaml
- run: bun test
```

### 4. lighthouse (optional)
```yaml
- uses: treosh/lighthouse-ci-action@v10
  with:
    urls: |
      http://localhost:3000/
    uploadArtifacts: true
```

## Triggers
- Push to main
- Pull requests to main

## Caching
```yaml
- uses: actions/cache@v3
  with:
    path: ~/.bun/install/cache
    key: ${{ runner.os }}-bun-${{ hashFiles('**/bun.lock') }}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Runs on every PR
- [ ] Lint + typecheck required to pass
- [ ] Build verification
- [ ] Caching for speed
- [ ] Clear failure messages","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-19T22:27:08.308979-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-20T11:48:52.669536-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-20T11:48:52.669536-05:00","close_reason":"Created GitHub Actions workflow for website CI. Fixed barrel import issues causing SSG build failures by switching to direct component imports. All lint, type-check, and build steps now pass.","labels":["ci","task"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-17e.1","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-17e","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-19T22:27:08.310651-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-17e.2","title":"Set up GitHub Actions for installer testing","description":"# Task: Set up GitHub Actions for Installer Testing

## Description
Create GitHub Actions workflow that tests the installer on real Ubuntu.

## Workflow File
`.github/workflows/installer.yml`

## Jobs

### 1. shellcheck
Fast job that runs on every PR:
```yaml
- uses: ludeeus/action-shellcheck@master
  with:
    scandir: './scripts'
    additional_files: 'install.sh'
```

### 2. test-ubuntu-25
Full installer test (may be slow):
```yaml
runs-on: ubuntu-latest
container:
  image: ubuntu:25.04
steps:
  - uses: actions/checkout@v4
  - name: Run installer
    run: |
      bash install.sh --yes --mode vibe
  - name: Run doctor
    run: |
      su - ubuntu -c "acfs doctor"
  - name: Verify key tools
    run: |
      su - ubuntu -c "ntm --help"
      su - ubuntu -c "ubs --help"
      # ... etc
```

### 3. test-ubuntu-24
Same but on 24.04 LTS for compatibility.

## Triggers
- Push to main
- Pull requests touching install.sh or scripts/

## Matrix Testing
```yaml
strategy:
  matrix:
    ubuntu: ['24.04', '25.04']
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] shellcheck passes on all scripts
- [ ] Installer works on Ubuntu 24.04
- [ ] Installer works on Ubuntu 25.04
- [ ] All tools verified after install
- [ ] acfs doctor exits 0","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-19T22:27:21.687302-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-20T12:46:57.410761-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-20T12:46:57.410761-05:00","close_reason":"Closed","labels":["ci","task"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-17e.2","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-17e","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-19T22:27:21.688994-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

 {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-5m6.8","title":"Create main install.sh orchestrator","description":"# Task: Create Main install.sh Orchestrator

## Description
Build the main installer script that orchestrates all phases.

## Script Structure

```bash
#!/bin/bash
set -euo pipefail

# Parse arguments
parse_args() {
    while [[ $# -gt 0 ]]; do
        case $1 in
            --yes) ACFS_AUTO_APPROVE=1 ;;
            --mode) ACFS_MODE="$2"; shift ;;
            --dry-run) ACFS_DRY_RUN=1 ;;
            --quiet) ACFS_QUIET=1 ;;
            --verbose) ACFS_VERBOSE=1 ;;
            *) log_error "Unknown option: $1"; exit 1 ;;
        esac
        shift
    done
}

# Main phases
main() {
    log_step "0/8" "Preflight checks"
    source_libs
    validate_os
    
    log_step "1/8" "Setting up ubuntu user"
    ensure_ubuntu_user
    setup_passwordless_sudo
    
    log_step "2/8" "Creating workspace"
    create_workspace
    
    log_step "3/8" "Installing shell environment"
    install_zsh_environment
    
    log_step "4/8" "Installing languages"
    install_languages
    
    log_step "5/8" "Installing dev tools"
    install_dev_tools
    
    log_step "6/8" "Installing coding agents"
    install_agents
    
    log_step "7/8" "Installing Dicklesworthstone stack"
    install_stack
    
    log_step "8/8" "Final configuration"
    deploy_configs
    install_onboard
    
    print_completion_message
}

main "$@"
```

## Error Handling
- Trap errors with helpful message
- Log to file before exit
- Suggest running acfs doctor

## Checkpointing
- Write completed phases to ~/.acfs/state.json
- Skip completed phases on re-run
- Support --force to re-run all

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All 8 phases implemented
- [ ] Clean error handling
- [ ] Checkpointing works
- [ ] --dry-run mode
- [ ] Completion message with next steps
- [ ] Works as root or ubuntu","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-19T22:25:56.003488-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-19T23:23:59.347345-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-19T23:23:59.347345-05:00","close_reason":"Files created in previous session: scripts/lib/logging.sh, os_detect.sh, user.sh, zsh.sh, and install.sh with all 8 phases","labels":["installer","task"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-5m6.8","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-5m6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-19T22:25:56.005402-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-5m6.9","title":"Create clean canonical acfs.zshrc","description":"# Task: Create Clean Canonical acfs.zshrc

## Description
Create the production-ready zshrc that ACFS deploys to VPS.

## File: acfs/zsh/acfs.zshrc

## Requirements
Based on user's original zshrc but:
- Remove duplicate PATH/Bun blocks
- Remove duplicate aliases
- Use safe chpwd hook instead of overriding cd()
- Fix atuin path before init ordering
- Conditional tool aliases (only if tool exists)
- Support user overrides via ~/.zshrc.local

## Structure

```bash
# ~/.acfs/zsh/acfs.zshrc
# ACFS canonical zsh config (managed)

# --- SSH guard ---
# Prevent weird terminal settings over SSH

# --- Paths (early) ---
# ~/.local/bin, ~/.cargo/bin, go, bun, atuin

# --- Oh My Zsh ---
# Theme: powerlevel10k
# Plugins: git, autosuggestions, syntax-highlighting, etc.

# --- Editor preference ---
# vim over SSH, nvim locally

# --- Modern CLI aliases ---
# Only set if tool exists
# lsd/eza, bat, fd, rg, dust, btop, nvim, lazygit

# --- Git aliases ---
# gs, gd, gdc, gp, gpu, gco, gcm, gca, gb, glog

# --- Docker aliases ---
# dc, dps, dpsa, dimg, dex

# --- Directory shortcuts ---
# dev, proj, dots, p

# --- Custom functions ---
# mkcd, extract

# --- Safe ls after cd ---
# Using chpwd hook, not overriding cd()

# --- Tool settings ---
# UV_LINK_MODE, etc.

# --- Tool initializations ---
# cargo, atuin, zoxide, direnv, fzf

# --- Agent aliases ---
# cc, cod, gmi (dangerous mode enabled)

# --- Keybindings ---
# Ctrl/Alt+Arrow for word movement
# Home/End keys
# Force atuin Ctrl-R
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] No duplicate code
- [ ] All tools alias only if present
- [ ] chpwd hook works correctly
- [ ] Atuin Ctrl-R works
- [ ] Local overrides supported
- [ ] Passes shellcheck","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-19T22:27:40.519614-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-20T11:59:34.896944-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-20T11:59:34.896944-05:00","close_reason":"Fixed shellcheck warnings, added directives for zsh-specific variables. File already met all requirements: no duplicates, conditional aliases, safe chpwd hook, proper atuin ordering, local overrides support.","labels":["config","task"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-5m6.9","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-5m6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-19T22:27:40.521482-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-5vz","title":"Add installer security verification","description":"# Task: Add Installer Security Verification

## Description
Make the curl|bash pattern more trustworthy.

## Features

### 1. Checksum Verification
Before running sub-installers, verify checksums:
```bash
verify_checksum() {
    local url="$1"
    local expected_sha256="$2"
    local content=$(curl -fsSL "$url")
    local actual=$(echo "$content" | sha256sum | cut -d' ' -f1)
    if [[ "$actual" != "$expected_sha256" ]]; then
        log_error "Checksum mismatch for $url"
        exit 1
    fi
    echo "$content"
}
```

### 2. HTTPS Enforcement
All URLs must be HTTPS. Fail on HTTP.

### 3. --print Mode
Show exactly what will be downloaded:
```bash
install.sh --print
# Outputs:
# Will download:
# - https://bun.sh/install (sha256: abc123...)
# - https://astral.sh/uv/install.sh (sha256: def456...)
# ...
```

### 4. Source Links
Website shows link to install.sh source on GitHub.

### 5. Changelog
What changed since last version.

## Checksums File
```yaml
# checksums.yaml
installers:
  bun: "abc123..."
  uv: "def456..."
  # etc
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Checksum verification for key installers
- [ ] HTTPS enforced
- [ ] --print mode works
- [ ] Website links to source","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-19T22:39:05.141855-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-20T12:52:21.63953-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-20T12:52:21.63953-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented security.sh with HTTPS enforcement, SHA256 checksum verification, --print/--verify/--update-checksums modes. Created checksums.yaml with hashes for 14 installers. Commit bc41158.","labels":["installer","security","task"]}

 {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-75r","title":"EPIC: acfs doctor & Update Commands","description":"# Epic: acfs doctor & Update Commands

## Overview
Build the diagnostic and update commands that help users verify and maintain their ACFS installation.

## Background & Reasoning
Beginners don't debug - they need clear "is it working?" feedback and simple fixes.

### acfs doctor
Single command to verify entire installation:
- Identity (user ubuntu, passwordless sudo)
- Shell setup (zsh, plugins, theme)
- All tools installed and working
- Clear PASS/FAIL/WARN for each check

### acfs update
Keep everything current:
- apt update + upgrade
- Update bun globals (codex, gemini, vercel, etc.)
- Optionally re-run tool installers

## Doctor Output Spec

```
ACFS Doctor v0.1.0
User: ubuntu ✅
Mode: vibe (passwordless sudo: enabled) ✅
OS: Ubuntu 25.x ✅

Shell
  PASS ✅ zsh installed (5.x)
  PASS ✅ oh-my-zsh installed
  ...

Dicklesworthstone stack
  PASS ✅ ntm (available)
  PASS ✅ slb (available)
  ...

Summary: 34 passed, 0 failed, 1 warning
Next: run `onboard`
```

## JSON Mode
`acfs doctor --json` outputs structured data for website integration:
```json
{
  "checks": [
    {"id": "identity.user_is_ubuntu", "status": "pass", ...}
  ],
  "summary": {"pass": 34, "warn": 1, "fail": 0}
}
```

## Success Criteria
- [ ] acfs doctor checks all ~40 tools
- [ ] Each check has stable ID for website mapping
- [ ] Exit code 0 = all pass, 1 = any fail
- [ ] --json mode for automation
- [ ] acfs update works without interaction
- [ ] Clear fix suggestions for each failure","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-19T22:21:55.795457-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-20T12:21:19.016509-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-20T12:21:19.016509-05:00","close_reason":"All sub-tasks completed: 75r.1 (acfs doctor) and 75r.2 (acfs update). Both commands fully implemented with comprehensive options.","labels":["epic","tooling"]}
 {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-75r.1","title":"Implement acfs doctor command","description":"# Task: Implement acfs doctor Command

## Description
Build the diagnostic command that verifies the entire ACFS installation.

## Location
Install to: `~/.acfs/bin/acfs`
Subcommand: `acfs doctor`

## Output Format

```
ACFS Doctor v0.1.0
User: ubuntu ✅
Mode: vibe (passwordless sudo: enabled) ✅
OS: Ubuntu 25.x ✅
Workspace: /data/projects ✅

Shell
  PASS ✅ zsh installed (zsh 5.x)
  PASS ✅ oh-my-zsh installed
  ...

Summary: 34 passed, 0 failed, 1 warning
Next: run `onboard`
```

## Check Structure

Each check has:
- id: Stable identifier for website mapping
- label: Human-readable name
- status: pass | warn | fail
- details: Version or extra info
- fix: Command to fix (on failure)

## Implementation

```bash
#!/bin/bash

run_check() {
    local id="$1"
    local label="$2"
    local check_cmd="$3"
    local fix_cmd="$4"
    
    if eval "$check_cmd" &>/dev/null; then
        echo "PASS ✅ $label"
        echo "{\"id\":\"$id\",\"status\":\"pass\"}" >> "$JSON_OUTPUT"
    else
        echo "FAIL ❌ $label"
        echo "      Fix: $fix_cmd"
        echo "{\"id\":\"$id\",\"status\":\"fail\",\"fix\":\"$fix_cmd\"}" >> "$JSON_OUTPUT"
        ((FAIL_COUNT++))
    fi
}

# Run all checks
run_check "identity.user_is_ubuntu" "Logged in as ubuntu" \
    "[[ \$(whoami) == 'ubuntu' ]]" \
    "sudo -u ubuntu bash"
```

## JSON Mode
`acfs doctor --json` outputs:
```json
{
  "acfs_version": "0.1.0",
  "checks": [...],
  "summary": {"pass": 34, "warn": 1, "fail": 0}
}
```

## Exit Codes
- 0: All pass (warnings OK)
- 1: Any failure
- 2: Doctor crashed

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All ~40 checks implemented
- [ ] Stable check IDs
- [ ] --json mode works
- [ ] Correct exit codes
- [ ] Fix suggestions on failure
- [ ] Summary at end","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-19T22:26:41.266691-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-20T12:11:02.667771-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-20T12:11:02.667771-05:00","close_reason":"scripts/lib/doctor.sh exists with 332 lines implementing comprehensive health checks","labels":["task","tooling"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-75r.1","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-75r","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-19T22:26:41.268261-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-75r.2","title":"Implement acfs update command","description":"# Task: Implement acfs update Command

## Description
Create command to update all ACFS-installed components.

## What It Updates

### System
```bash
sudo apt update && sudo apt upgrade -y && sudo apt autoremove -y
```

### Bun Globals
```bash
bun install -g @openai/codex@latest
bun install -g @google/gemini-cli@latest
bun install -g wrangler@latest
bun install -g supabase@latest
bun install -g vercel@latest
```

### Claude Code
```bash
claude update
```

### Dicklesworthstone Stack
- Check for updates to each tool
- Re-run installers if --force flag

## Flags
- --apt-only: Only system packages
- --agents-only: Only coding agents
- --stack-only: Only Dicklesworthstone tools
- --force: Re-run all installers even if up-to-date

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Updates apt packages
- [ ] Updates bun globals
- [ ] Updates Claude Code
- [ ] Optional stack update
- [ ] Progress output
- [ ] Summary of what was updated","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-19T22:38:14.55906-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-20T12:20:44.214258-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-20T12:20:44.214258-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented acfs update command with: apt updates, bun self-upgrade, agent updates (Claude/Codex/Gemini), cloud CLI updates (Wrangler/Supabase/Vercel), Rust/uv updates, optional stack updates.  {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-vqr","title":"Create SSH troubleshooting guide","description":"# Task: Create SSH Troubleshooting Guide

## Description
Comprehensive guide for common SSH connection failures.

## Location
- Website: Embedded in wizard step 6
- Installer: ~/.acfs/docs/ssh_troubleshooting.md

## Common Issues Covered

### Permission denied (publickey)
**Symptoms:** `Permission denied (publickey)`
**Causes:**
1. Wrong key file specified
2. Key not added to VPS
3. Wrong username

**Fixes:**
```bash
# Verify key exists
ls -la ~/.ssh/acfs_ed25519*

# Try with verbose output
ssh -v -i ~/.ssh/acfs_ed25519 ubuntu@IP
```

### Connection refused
**Symptoms:** `Connection refused`
**Causes:**
1. SSH service not running
2. Firewall blocking port 22
3. Wrong IP address

**Fixes:**
- Check IP is correct
- VPS provider may need firewall rule

### Connection timeout
**Symptoms:** `Connection timed out`
**Causes:**
1. Wrong IP
2. VPS not fully booted
3. Network issue

**Fixes:**
- Wait 2-3 minutes for VPS to boot
- Verify IP in provider dashboard

### Host key verification failed
**Symptoms:** `Host key verification failed`
**Causes:** 
- VPS was recreated with same IP
- Man-in-the-middle warning

**Fixes:**
```bash
ssh-keygen -R IP_ADDRESS
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All common issues documented
- [ ] Clear cause → fix structure
- [ ] Commands are copy-paste ready

 {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-yad","title":"EPIC: Website Wizard MVP","description":"# Epic: Website Wizard MVP

## Overview
Build the step-by-step wizard website that guides beginners from "I have a laptop" to "paste the curl|bash command".

## Background & Reasoning
The wizard is the user's first touchpoint with ACFS. It must:
- Feel premium and trustworthy
- Never overwhelm with jargon
- Make every step feel achievable
- Handle both Mac and Windows users
- Guide through VPS provider selection

## Tech Stack
- Next.js 16 App Router
- Bun runtime
- Tailwind CSS + shadcn/ui
- Vercel hosting + Cloudflare caching

## Key Design Principles
1. **Single action per screen** - One button or one command
2. **Copy button on every command** - Reduce friction
3. **"I did it" checkbox** - Gates "Next" button
4. **Collapsible troubleshooting** - Don't clutter but be ready

## Success Criteria
- [ ] All 10 wizard steps implemented
- [ ] OS-aware command rendering
- [ ] Mobile responsive
- [ ] Lighthouse score > 90
- [ ] Works without JavaScript (progressive enhancement)

## Relationship to Project Goals
The wizard is the "front door" to ACFS. A confused or frustrated user at this stage will never reach the VPS installer. Excellence here directly enables the 10-minute promise.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-19T22:20:46.959703-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-20T11:25:29.453083-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-20T11:25:29.453083-05:00","close_reason":"All 15 wizard steps implemented and verified. Epic complete.","labels":["epic","website"]}
 {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-yad.1","title":"Initialize Next.js 16 App Router project","description":"# Task: Initialize Next.js 16 App Router Project

## Description
Set up the Next.js 16 project in apps/web/ with:
- App Router structure
- TypeScript configuration
- Tailwind CSS
- shadcn/ui components

## Technical Details
```bash
cd apps/web
bunx create-next-app@latest . --typescript --tailwind --eslint --app --src-dir
bunx shadcn-ui@latest init
```

## Configuration
- Use Bun as package manager
- Configure for Vercel deployment
- Set up path aliases (@/components, @/lib)
- Configure next.config.js for optimal settings

## Reasoning
Next.js 16 with App Router provides:
- Modern React Server Components
- Automatic code splitting
- Optimal performance defaults
- Easy Vercel deployment

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Next.js 16 project created
- [ ] TypeScript strict mode enabled
- [ ] Tailwind CSS working
- [ ] shadcn/ui initialized
- [ ] bun run dev works
- [ ] bun run build succeeds","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-19T22:22:18.88745-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-19T23:27:32.977854-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-19T23:27:32.977854-05:00","close_reason":"Next.js 16 project initialized with TypeScript, Tailwind CSS v4, shadcn/ui, and turbopack.  {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-yad.10","title":"Implement wizard Step 5: Create VPS and Attach SSH Key","description":"# Task: Wizard Step 5 - Create VPS

## Description
Guide users through VPS creation with provider-specific instructions.

## Key Elements
- Universal checklist (select Ubuntu, paste SSH key, create, copy IP)
- Provider-specific collapsible sections with screenshots
- IP address input field (validated, stored in localStorage)

## Why This Is Its Own Step
This is the most variable step - different providers have different UIs.
Screenshots and specific guidance needed for each.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Universal checklist clear
- [ ] Provider sections expandable
- [ ] IP input with validation
- [ ] Screenshot placeholders ready","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-19T22:37:06.118022-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-19T23:54:02.512884-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-19T23:54:02.512884-05:00","close_reason":"Closed","labels":["mvp","task","website","wizard-step"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-yad.10","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-yad","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-19T22:37:06.120067-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

@@ -0,0 +1,120 @@
+#!/usr/bin/env bash
+# ============================================================
+# ACFS Installer - Ubuntu Integration Test (Docker)
+#
+# Runs the full installer inside a fresh Ubuntu container image, then runs
+# `acfs doctor` as the `ubuntu` user.
+#
+# Usage:
+#   ./tests/vm/test_install_ubuntu.sh              # defaults to 24.04
+#   ./tests/vm/test_install_ubuntu.sh --all        # run 24.04 + 25.04
+#   ./tests/vm/test_install_ubuntu.sh --ubuntu 25.04
+#
+# Requirements:
+#   - docker (or compatible runtime that supports `docker run`)
+# ============================================================
+
+set -euo pipefail
+
+usage() {
+  cat <<'EOF'
+tests/vm/test_install_ubuntu.sh - ACFS installer integration test (Docker)
+
+Usage:
+  ./tests/vm/test_install_ubuntu.sh [options]
+
+Options:
+  --ubuntu <version>   Ubuntu tag (e.g. 24.04, 25.04). Repeatable.
+  --all                Run on 24.04 and 25.04.
+  --help               Show help.
+
+Examples:
+  ./tests/vm/test_install_ubuntu.sh
+  ./tests/vm/test_install_ubuntu.sh --all
+  ./tests/vm/test_install_ubuntu.sh --ubuntu 25.04
+EOF
+}
+
+if [[ "${1:-}" == "--help" ]]; then
+  usage
+  exit 0
+fi
+
+if ! command -v docker >/dev/null 2>&1; then
+  echo "ERROR: docker not found. Install Docker Desktop or docker engine." >&2
+  exit 1
+fi
+
+SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
+REPO_ROOT="$(cd "$SCRIPT_DIR/../.." && pwd)"
+
+declare -a ubuntus=()
+while [[ $# -gt 0 ]]; do
+  case "$1" in
+    --ubuntu)
+      ubuntus+=("${2:-}")
+      shift 2
+      ;;
+    --all)
+      ubuntus=("24.04" "25.04")
+      shift
+      ;;
+    --help)
+      usage
+      exit 0
+      ;;
+    *)
+      echo "Unknown option: $1" >&2
+      usage >&2
+      exit 1
+      ;;
+  esac
+done
+
+if [[ ${#ubuntus[@]} -eq 0 ]]; then
+  ubuntus=("24.04")
+fi
+
+run_one() {
+  local ubuntu_version="$1"
+  local image="ubuntu:${ubuntu_version}"
+
+  echo "" >&2
+  echo "============================================================" >&2
+  echo "[ACFS Test] Ubuntu ${ubuntu_version}" >&2
+  echo "============================================================" >&2
+
+  docker pull "$image" >/dev/null
+
+  docker run --rm -t \
+    -e DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive \
+    -v "${REPO_ROOT}:/repo:ro" \
+    "$image" bash -lc '
+      set -euo pipefail
+
+      apt-get update
+      apt-get install -y sudo curl git ca-certificates jq unzip tar xz-utils gnupg
+
+      cd /repo
+      bash install.sh --yes --mode vibe
+
+      su - ubuntu -c "zsh -ic '\''acfs doctor'\''"
+      su - ubuntu -c "zsh -ic '\''onboard --help >/dev/null'\''"
+      su - ubuntu -c "zsh -ic '\''ntm --help >/dev/null'\''"
+      su - ubuntu -c "zsh -ic '\''codex --version >/dev/null'\''"
+      su - ubuntu -c "zsh -ic '\''gemini --version >/dev/null'\''"
+      su - ubuntu -c "zsh -ic '\''claude --version >/dev/null'\''"
+    '
+}
+
+for ubuntu_version in "${ubuntus[@]}"; do
+  if [[ -z "$ubuntu_version" ]]; then
+    echo "ERROR: --ubuntu requires a version (e.g. 24.04)" >&2
+    exit 1
+  fi
+  run_one "$ubuntu_version"
+done
+
+echo "" >&2
+echo "✅ All requested Ubuntu installer tests passed." >&2
+
